subject: Statistics
title: The Salk Polio Vaccine Trial
description: A fast breakdown of The Salk Polio Vaccine Trial for builders.

hook.text: In 1954, 1.8 million children participated in the largest medical experiment in history.

buildup.text: Jonas Salk's polio vaccine was tested in a massive double-blind, placebo-controlled randomized trial.

discovery.text: The vaccine reduced paralytic polio by 80-90%. The result was announced to a cheering nation on April 12, 1955.

twist.text: An earlier, non-randomized version of the trial gave misleading results due to volunteer bias. Design matters.

climax.text: The Salk trial proved that large-scale randomized experiments could answer life-or-death medical questions.

punchline.text: 1.8 million children. One well-designed trial. Polio was conquered.

quiz.question: What problem occurred with the initial non-randomized version of the Salk vaccine trial?
options: Volunteer bias distorted the results — the randomized design was needed for reliable evidence
The vaccine didn't work in the non-randomized trial
